content:
Am I seeing it correctly that we can currently only use the system_wq in Rust?

Is anyone working on creating own workqueues. @Alice Ryhl you mabe?
I have this patch, but I need to pick it up again.

Right now you can use any of the built-in workqueues, so you can use system_wq system_bh_wq system_bh_highpri_wq system_freezable_wq system_freezable_power_efficient_wq system_highpri_wq system_long_wq system_power_efficient_wq system_unbound_wq
OK. Is there any timeline for that; do you need help?

I remember running into the need for specific workqueues, back when I was working on unified memory (page faulting for GPU and system memory, and migrating pages back and forth between GPU and CPU memory).

The system workqueue couldn't be used because it would have lead to deadlocks. For complex drivers, this is going to be routinely required.

I expect that Nova will eventually need it, although I might be wrong, because we'll be layering on top of several subsystems that already work well (HMM, DRM), so we'll see.
I'll send another version of it. Reviews would be great when it arrives.
I'll be glad to help review that one.
